title: The Phish Finder 🎣
description: Identify the 3 main red flags in a fake 'Bank Alert' email.
problemStatement:
You receive the following email:

**From**: `security-alert@bank-of-america-verification.com`  
**Subject**: URGENT: YOUR ACCOUNT IS LOCKED !!!  
**Content**: Dear valued user, our systems detect a suspicious login. To avoid account deletion, click [here](http://login-verification-secure.net/fix) within 2 minutes.  

**Your Mission**: List the 3 most obvious red flags in this email.
hints: Look closely at the sender's email domain.
Check the tone of the subject line.
Hover (in your mind) over the 'here' link.
expectedOutput:
1. Suspicious sender domain (not bankofamerica.com)
2. Extreme urgency and threats (deletion in 2 mins)
3. The link doesn't go to the official bank website
solutionCode:
1. Suspicious sender domain
2. Urgency/Threats
3. Fake link destination
